I support this rule's goals. CMS is right to hold OPOs accountable. But I write to identify a critical gap: the rule establishes rigorous performance accountability without addressing the technological infrastructure OPOs need to actually meet the standards being imposed. Under this system even if every OPO were operating at a high level tomorrow, half would still be classified as underperforming and subject to penalties. This approach prioritizes relative ranking over meaningful, objective benchmarks and ultimately undermines the goal of system-wide progress.
